evals.prompt: Hey, PR #475 in canonical/haproxy-operator was generated by Copilot and just merged. There were quite a few review comments that changed the implementation. Can you analyze it and update our best practices guide?
evals.expected_output: Creates or updates .github/instructions/best-practices.instructions.md with themed do/don't rules derived from the diff between the first and last commit of PR #475. Each rule cites PR #475.

assertions.name: creates-output-file
assertions.description: best-practices.instructions.md is created in the output directory

assertions.name: has-valid-frontmatter
assertions.description: File starts with YAML frontmatter block containing applyTo field

assertions.name: has-themed-sections
assertions.description: File contains at least 2 theme headings (## level)

assertions.name: has-dos-and-donts
assertions.description: File contains both ### Do and ### Don't sections

assertions.name: cites-pr-number
assertions.description: At least one rule cites PR #475

assertions.name: rules-are-specific
assertions.description: No rule bullet is shorter than 20 characters (not vague stubs)

assertions.name: has-code-examples
assertions.description: Each theme contains at least one fenced code block (triple backtick) showing a concrete before/after from the diff

evals.prompt: I just merged PR #500 in canonical/haproxy-operator - it was written by an AI coding agent. There were several rounds of review that changed the validation logic and error handling. Please run the pr-best-practices skill to extract lessons and add them to the instructions file.
evals.expected_output: Creates or updates .github/instructions/best-practices.instructions.md with rules about validation and error handling extracted from PR #500's first-vs-final diff. Does not duplicate existing rules.

assertions.name: creates-output-file
assertions.description: best-practices.instructions.md is created in the output directory

assertions.name: cites-pr-500
assertions.description: At least one rule cites PR #500

assertions.name: covers-validation-or-error-handling
assertions.description: At least one theme section covers validation or error handling patterns

assertions.name: has-code-examples
assertions.description: Each theme contains at least one fenced code block (triple backtick) showing a concrete before/after from the diff

evals.prompt: PR #495 just merged in canonical/haproxy-operator — it was a bot-generated change. Can you analyze it with the pr-best-practices skill and see if there's anything to learn?
evals.expected_output: Detects that PR #495 (Change prometheus metrics port from 9123 to 8404) is a trivial single-commit change with no significant first-vs-final diff and reports there is nothing actionable to extract. Does not modify best-practices.instructions.md.

assertions.name: no-best-practices-file-created
assertions.description: Does NOT create best-practices.instructions.md (trivial diff, nothing to extract)

assertions.name: reports-non-significance
assertions.description: Summary explicitly states the diff is trivial / not significant / nothing actionable to extract
